name: Vald
tagline: Kubernetes-native distributed engine for billion-scale approximate nearest neighbor search
seoTitle: Vald: Distributed Vector Search on Kubernetes
metaDescription: Vald is a Kubernetes-native distributed approximate nearest neighbor (ANN) vector search engine built on NGT, with auto-indexing, backup, and horizontal scaling.
overview: Vald is a distributed approximate nearest neighbor (ANN) search engine for dense vectors. It is built on a cloud-native architecture and runs on Kubernetes, so it is designed to search across very large datasets, up to billions of feature vectors.

It handles automatic vector indexing, index backup, and horizontal scaling, and uses the NGT algorithm to find neighbors. Any data you can turn into a vector, such as images, audio, video, or text, can be indexed and searched.

Vald fits the vector-database category as a self-hosted option for teams already running Kubernetes. It is a good match for recommendation, recognition, and similar-search workloads where you need to scale the index across a cluster rather than a single node.
features: Distributed ANN search built on the NGT algorithm for nearest-neighbor lookups
Cloud-native design that runs on Kubernetes (1.19+) and scales horizontally
Automatic vector indexing and index backup
Component-based architecture: Agent NGT, Discoverer, Gateways, and Index Manager
Installable with Helm or the Vald Helm operator
Official client SDKs for Go, Java, Python, and Node.js

gettingStarted.intro: Vald runs as a set of components on a Kubernetes cluster. Install it with Helm, then connect from one of the official client SDKs to insert and search vectors.

steps.heading: Check the requirements
steps.body: You need Kubernetes 1.19 or later, and the Vald Agent NGT requires CPUs with AVX2 instructions.

steps.heading: Install with Helm
steps.body: Add the Vald chart repository and install the cluster. Using the default values.yaml installs the nightly images.
steps.code:
helm repo add vald https://vald.vdaas.org/charts
helm install vald-cluster vald/vald

steps.heading: Install a client SDK
steps.body: Vald ships official clients for Go, Java, Python, and Node.js. This example uses the Python client.

steps.heading: Insert and search vectors
steps.body: Connect to the Vald gateway over gRPC, insert a vector, then run a nearest-neighbor search.
steps.code:
import grpc
from vald.v1.vald import insert_pb2_grpc, search_pb2_grpc
from vald.v1.payload import payload_pb2

channel = grpc.insecure_channel("gateway.vald.vdaas.org:80")
istub = insert_pb2_grpc.InsertStub(channel)
sstub = search_pb2_grpc.SearchStub(channel)

vec = payload_pb2.Object.Vector(id='vector_id_1', vector=[0.1, 0.2, 0.3])
icfg = payload_pb2.Insert.Config(skip_strict_exist_check=True)
istub.Insert(payload_pb2.Insert.Request(vector=vec, config=icfg))

scfg = payload_pb2.Search.Config(num=10, radius=-1.0, epsilon=0.01, timeout=3000000000)
res = sstub.Search(payload_pb2.Search.Request(vector=[0.1, 0.2, 0.3], config=scfg))

channel.close()

useCases: Power a recommendation system that finds similar items from billions of embeddings
Build image, audio, or video similarity search by indexing model embeddings
Run semantic text search and retrieval over large document collections
Self-host a scalable vector index on an existing Kubernetes cluster
